lop: verb. To cut off as the top or extreme part of anything, especially to prune a small limb off a shrub or tree, or sometimes to behead
loppy: adjective. Somewhat lop; inclined to lop; droopy; floppy
lory: noun. Any of various small, brightly coloured parrots native to Australasia.
lot: noun. A large quantity or number; a great deal.
molt: verb. US standard spelling of moult.
moly: noun. A magic herb or plant used by Odysseus to overcome Circe.
mop: noun. An implement for washing floors or similar, made of a piece of cloth, or a collection of thrums, or coarse yarn, fastened to a handle.
mor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Moro.
mort: noun. Death; especially, the death of game in hunting.
mot: noun. A witty remark; a witticism; a bon mot.
ort: noun. A fragment; a scrap of leftover food; any remainder; a piece of refuse.
plop: noun. A sound or action like liquid hitting a hard surface, or an object falling into a body of water.
plot: noun. The course of a story, comprising a series of incidents which are gradually unfolded, sometimes by unexpected means.
ploy: noun. A tactic, strategy, or scheme.
polyp: noun. A cylindrical coelenterate, such as the hydra, having a mouth surrounded with tentacles.
pom: noun. An Englishman; a Briton; a person of British descent.
pomp: noun. Show of magnificence; parade; display; power.
pop: noun. A loud, sharp sound, as of a cork coming out of a bottle, especially when the contents are pressurized by fizziness.
port: noun. A place on the coast at which ships can shelter, or dock to load and unload cargo or passengers.
pot: noun. A flat - bottomed vessel (usually metal) used for cooking food, possibly excluding saucepans (see usage notes).
pro: noun. An advantage of something, especially when contrasted with its disadvantages (cons).
prop: noun. An object placed against or under another, to support it; anything that supports.
propyl: noun. Either of two isomeric univalent hydrocarbon radicals, C 3 H 7, formally derived from propane by the loss of a hydrogen atom.
pry: verb. To peer closely and curiously, especially at something closed or not public.
rom: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Romani.
romp: verb. To play about roughly, energetically or boisterously.
ropy: adjective. Resembling rope in appearance or texture, used especially of muscles that are thick or hard to the touch.
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
rotl: noun. Alternative form of rottol: a former Middle Eastern and North African unit of weight, usually 1–5 pounds (0.5–2.5 kg).
ryot: noun. A farmer or tiller of the soil.
tom: noun. The male of the domesticated cat, especially if not neutered.
top: noun. The highest or uppermost part of something.
tor: noun. A craggy outcrop of rock on the summit of a hill, created by the erosion and weathering of rock.
tory: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Tory.
toy: noun. Something to play with, especially as intended for use by a child.
tromp: verb. To tread heavily, especially to crush underfoot.
trop: noun. Alternative form of trope (“cantillation pattern”).
troy: adjective. Of, or relating to, troy weight.
yom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yombe.
lor: particle. Tagged at the end of a sentence to convey a sense of resignation or inevitability.
loy: noun. A type of one-eared spade used in Ireland.
olm: noun. Proteus anguinus, a cave - dwelling neotenous salamander with external gills, found along the coast from northeastern Italy to
ory: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Odia.
por: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Portuguese.
porty: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of port wine.
prp: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Parsi.
pyot: noun. A magpie; a water ouzel.
rompy: adjective. Tending to romp; playfully adventurous.
tlo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Talodi.
tlr: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Talise.
topply: adjective. Tending to topple; unstable and ready to overturn.
trm: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tregami.
trp: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kokborok.
tymp: noun. A hollow water-cooled iron casting in the upper part of the archway of a blast furnace in which the dam stands.
yomp: noun. A long-distance march carrying full kit.
yor: adjective. Eye dialect spelling of your.
yot: noun. The letter Ϳ ϳ, an uncommon variant of J j used in Greek linguistics.
